summary: Both backend and frontend tested end-to-end for the Hindi-Kannada Bhasha Bridge translator. Backend: 10/10 pytest cases passed including health, HI->KN and KN->HI text translation (gpt-5.2 via Emergent LLM key returning proper Devanagari/Kannada script), empty-text 400 validation, invalid-lang 422, conversations GET without _id, DELETE clearing, and translate-audio empty/invalid payload error handling. Frontend (Expo web preview, mobile viewport 390x844): split-screen loaded with Kannada panel rotated 180deg on top, Hindi panel on bottom, divider labeled 'Bhasha Bridge'. All required testIDs present. Hindi Aa->text-input->submit produced correct Kannada (ನಮಸ್ತೆ ಸ್ನೇಹಿತ) in top panel with replay-btn-kn; Kannada Aa flow produced correct Hindi (नमस्कार मित्र) in bottom panel with replay-btn-hi. History modal showed both entries with per-entry Play buttons; clear-history-btn emptied the list and backend DELETE was called. No red screens, no console errors (only expo shadow-prop deprecation warnings).

issues: Kannada glyphs render as disconnected shapes in the web preview screenshot - the browser rendering environment lacks a proper Kannada OpenType font with shaping. Actual DOM text content is correct, so this is a web-only font-loading concern (native iOS/Android will render correctly). Main agent may optionally bundle a Kannada webfont (e.g., Noto Sans Kannada) for web builds.

react_native_issues.issue: Uses deprecated shadow* props triggering RN web warnings. Non-blocking. Consider migrating to boxShadow.

action_items: No blocking fixes needed. Optional: add Noto Sans Kannada / Noto Sans Devanagari webfont for crisp Indic rendering in web preview.
Optional: migrate shadow* styles on micBtn to boxShadow to silence RN web deprecation warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: logger is defined AFTER the route handlers that reference it (logger.exception in except blocks). It still works because the except branches only execute at request time (after module load completes), but it is fragile. Move logging.basicConfig + logger definition to the top of the file, right after imports.
server.py: LLM-produced translations are trusted verbatim. Consider stripping wrapping quotes/backticks defensively if gpt-5.2 occasionally returns them.
index.tsx: when stopRecording runs on web without chunks, it still POSTs an empty-ish blob. Consider guarding when mediaChunksRef is empty to surface a friendlier error.
index.tsx: File is ~917 lines but cleanly organized into parent + LanguagePanel; acceptable for now.
seed_data_creation: Tests create and then DELETE all conversations (test_clear_conversations).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Microphone recording could not be exercised in the automated browser (no real mic). Text-input translation flow fully covers backend integration. If audio testing is required, create a small valid webm/m4a sample and POST to /api/translate-audio; Whisper via emergentintegrations expects file.name set (already handled).
